_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the narrative skill: does a real `/charness:narrative` run honor its central claim — route to its references at the point of need? narrative has no planner that emits required reads; its bootstrap scripts resolve the adapter (resolve_adapter.py / review_adapter.py) and map the source surface (map_sources.py emits the source_documents set + git freshness). RCF is derived from the SKILL.md workflow plus script-resolution analysis (lens 3/7). Two of the three formerly-engage-always docs are script-resolved and so are engage-always but NOT RCF floors: adapter-contract.md (resolve_adapter.py / review_adapter.py resolve the adapter; the run reads the resolved adapter, not the explainer) and source-map.md (map_sources.py resolves the source-document set + freshness; the run reads the script's output, and the alignment rules are inlined in SKILL.md step 5) — claimRole script-resolved-context, the same shape as hotl's adapter-contract.md. The single engage-always floor is brief-shape.md: it owns the audience-neutral discipline and the what-to-avoid rules (do not bake in one audience/channel, do not dump repo paths / decision-ids / stage-labels, do not replace source docs) that no script resolves and the adapter's brief_template does not supply (the adapter owns only the ordered skeleton). This calibration shipped a skill fix in the same pass: SKILL.md step 6 previously INLINED the brief Core Rule without routing to brief-shape.md at the point of need (the doc was only in the bottom References list, while the on-demand docs landing-rewrite-loop / cross-repo-issue-shaping / scenario-blocks WERE routed at their steps) — an unsatisfiable floor, the same defect ideation had. Step 6 now reads 'per references/brief-shape.md', making the floor real. The prompt is pinned to a representative truth-surface alignment that derives a brief (bare /charness:narrative needs a narrative goal/target, lens 2 + lens 8); charness ships a narrative adapter (.agents/narrative-adapter.yaml with source_documents + brief_template), so the run does not block at the adapter seam and reaches step 6 where brief-shape.md is forced. The prompt names neither the reference file nor the what-to-avoid rules (ungameable: the matcher scores file-opens, not prompt wording). declaredReferences is the full skill-local set (coverage); shared refs (agent-assessment-invariant.md, closeout-discipline.md, success-criteria-review.md) are not skill-local so they are not declared. referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract. thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget.
prompt: /charness:narrative 이 repo의 source-of-truth 문서(README·docs/control-plane 등 어댑터가 선언한 truth surface)를 현재 스토리에 맞게 정렬하고, 모순·stale 가정을 surface한 뒤, announcement가 나중에 각색할 수 있는 audience-neutral brief skeleton을 도출해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Step 2 reviews adapter fitness on every run, but resolve_adapter.py and review_adapter.py resolve the adapter (source set, mutable documents, brief_template, block/warn findings) — the run reads the resolved adapter output, not this explainer doc, so it is engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved).

brief-shape.md.rationale: The single engage-always floor. Workflow step 6 now derives the brief 'per references/brief-shape.md'; the doc owns the audience-neutral discipline and the what-to-avoid rules (do not dump repo paths / decision-ids / stage-labels, do not replace source docs) that no script resolves and the adapter's brief_template does not supply. A representative run that derives a brief and never opens it skipped its own routing; the same-pass skill fix added the point-of-need route so this floor is real.

cross-repo-issue-shaping.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 4 routes to this reference only for cross-repo issues, ensuring the writing order keeps why-what-how honest instead of leading with implementation.
cross-repo-issue-shaping.md.trigger: The work is shaped as a cross-repo issue, proposal, or durable note that will be handed to another repo rather than solved only inside the current one.

landing-rewrite-loop.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 4 explicitly routes to this reference only when rewriting a high-leverage first-touch surface, providing the detailed loop for landing-page specific work.
landing-rewrite-loop.md.trigger: The target is a high-leverage first-touch surface (README, landing page, operator doc) requiring the full loop of comparables, intent inventory, claim audit, and compression metrics.

scenario-blocks.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 5 routes to this reference only when adding scenario blocks for products with multiple use-case paths, using the relevant subset of slot templates.
scenario-blocks.md.trigger: The repo has multiple first-class use cases or the adapter declares scenario_surfaces that need to stay visible in durable docs.

source-map.md.rationale: Step 2 maps the truth surface on every run, but map_sources.py resolves the source-document set, dirty paths, and git freshness, and SKILL.md step 5 inlines the alignment rules (rewrite contradictions at source, one durable narrative) — the run reads the script's output. The one rule this doc uniquely holds that neither the script emits nor SKILL.md inlines is the freshness interpretation 'remote-ahead does not force a stop; stop when local truth cannot be trusted', which is load-bearing only in the narrow remote-ahead case (charness is current), so this stays engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved).
